body.title: [sprint] Redact secrets from the every-byte inference corpus (training-log vs no-persisted-secret collision)
body.description:
**Problem (audit-expert Security lens, 2026-07-05):** io_corpus.py (shipped this week) persists the COMPLETE inference request (system prompt + messages + tool args) and raw response to plaintext JSONL under uu_home, per the hard rule 'every byte logged for training'. This COLLIDES with 'no secret in a persisted value' ([[compose-credentials-at-connect-time]]): if any inference request ever carries a connect-time credential, vault value, or token-in-context, it is now written permanently in cleartext to the corpus.

**Proposed shape:** a redaction pass at capture time in io_corpus.capture() — scrub known secret shapes (API-key regexes, vault-composed values, .env token patterns, Authorization headers) before the JSON line is written; OR a documented+enforced guarantee that no secret ever crosses the inference boundary (with a test). Prefer defense-in-depth: both. Keep fail-soft (redaction error must not break dispatch).

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/io_corpus.py (capture()), possibly a shared secret-shape scrubber.

**Design rules:** fail-soft (never break dispatch); the corpus stays greppable; redaction must be deterministic (no wall-clock/random).

**Scope boundary:** capture-time redaction of secret-shaped substrings ONLY. NOT re-scrubbing the existing corpus (separate if needed), NOT a full DLP system.

**Completion criteria:** a request carrying an API-key-shaped token is written to the corpus with the token redacted; a normal request is unchanged.

**Test plan:** hermetic tests/inference/ — capture a record whose request contains a fake API-key-shaped token; read the corpus file; assert the token is redacted and the surrounding content intact. PROOF NODE: the secret-shaped token is absent from the written line where an unredacted capture would contain it. red→green via proof_emitter.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
